steps.observed: Help output lists all 4 required flags plus optional --output, --seed, --data, --river, --start, --end, --export-winner
search.py: error: the following arguments are required: --iterations (exit code 2)
search.py: error: the following arguments are required: --config (exit code 2)

steps.action: Run search.py with no flags at all
steps.expected: Argparse error about missing required flags
steps.observed: search.py: error: the following arguments are required: --config (exit code 2)

evidence.terminalOutput: --help shows all 4 required flags. Missing flags produce clear argparse errors with usage message and exit code 2.

steps.action: Run python3 search.py --config configs/locked_baseline.yaml --search-space configs/search_space.yaml --labels site/data/labels/ --data data/eurusd_1m_2024-01-07_to_2024-01-12.csv --iterations 3 --output /tmp/search-test-1.json --seed 42
steps.expected: Exactly 3 iterations run with progress displayed per iteration
steps.observed: Output shows [1/3], [2/3], [3/3] progress lines. JSON output has iterations_completed=3 and 3 candidates.

evidence.terminalOutput: Progress lines: [1/3] score=0.0000 best=0.0000 improvement=+0.0000, [2/3] ..., [3/3] ..., Summary: Iterations: 3/3
evidence.jsonOutput: metadata.iterations_completed=3, metadata.iterations_requested=3, candidates array has 3 entries

steps.expected: JSON with candidates array sorted by fitness descending. Each entry has rank, score, config, iteration.
steps.observed: Top-level keys: schema_version, metadata, candidates. 3 candidates with ranks [1,2,3]. Each has rank, score, config (dict of param paths to values), iteration number.

steps.action: Validate candidates are sorted by rank ascending (fitness descending)
Check each candidate has required fields
steps.expected: rank, score, config, iteration present in every candidate
steps.observed: All 3 candidates have rank, score, config, iteration. Also have param_deltas, precision, recall, delta_from_baseline, kept.

evidence.jsonOutput: schema_version=1.0, candidates[0]={rank:1, score:0.0, iteration:1, config:{5 params}}, candidates[1]={rank:2, ...}, candidates[2]={rank:3, ...}

steps.action: Launch search with --iterations 20 and send SIGINT after ~10s (during iteration 3)
steps.expected: SIGINT saves completed iterations to output file. No data loss or corrupt JSON.
steps.observed: Interrupt message displayed: '⚠ Interrupt received — finishing current iteration and saving results...'. Current iteration (3) completed, then saved. Summary shows 'Search INTERRUPTED'.

steps.expected: Valid JSON with completed iterations saved
steps.observed: Valid JSON with 3 candidates. metadata.interrupted=true, metadata.iterations_completed=3, metadata.iterations_requested=20.

evidence.terminalOutput: ⚠ Interrupt received — finishing current iteration and saving results... [3/20] score=0.0000 ... Interrupted — saving 3 completed iterations ... Search INTERRUPTED ... Results saved to: /tmp/search-test-sigint.json

assertions.title: Progress display with best score tracking

steps.expected: Each iteration shows [N/total], current score, best score so far, improvement indicator. Summary block after completion.
steps.observed: Each iteration: [N/3] score=X.XXXX best=Y.YYYY improvement=+Z.ZZZZ. Summary block: ====== Search COMPLETE ====== Iterations: 3/3, Baseline score, Best score (iteration N), Improvement, Duration.

steps.expected: Summary with total iterations, best score, improvement from baseline, duration
steps.observed: Summary: Iterations: 3/3, Baseline score: 0.0000, Best score: 0.0000 (iteration 0), Improvement: +0.0000 (+0.0%), Duration: 8.2s

steps.action: Check all perturbed values in 3 output files against search_space.yaml min/max bounds
steps.expected: No value exceeds defined min/max for any parameter
steps.observed: All values within bounds across all 3 files (9 candidates total). Example: displacement.ltf.atr_multiplier bounds [1.0, 3.0], values seen: 1.25, 1.75 — all within bounds.
pct_change values: -16.67%, +16.67%, +8.33%, +33.33% — perturbations are within expected range and values clamped to bounds

evidence.terminalOutput:
PASS [/tmp/search-test-1.json]: All perturbed values within bounds
PASS [/tmp/search-test-2.json]: All perturbed values within bounds
PASS [/tmp/search-test-3.json]: All perturbed values within bounds
Detailed: atr_multiplier 1.25 in [1.0,3.0], body_ratio 0.5 in [0.4,0.85], confirmation_window_bars 3 in [1,8], N.15m 2 in [1,6], N.5m 3 in [2,8]

steps.observed: No categorical parameters in production search_space.yaml — all 5 params are numeric type

steps.action: Test categorical perturbation code path directly via module import with synthetic categorical param
steps.expected: Categorical values only come from defined options list
steps.observed: Using _perturb_categorical with options ['optionA','optionB','optionC'] over 20 seeds: all generated values {'optionA','optionB','optionC'} are within options. Multiple options selected (not stuck on one).

evidence.terminalOutput: All values generated: {'optionC', 'optionA', 'optionB'}\nPASS: All categorical values are from the defined options list\nPASS: Multiple different categorical values selected
evidence.note: Production search_space.yaml has no categorical params. Code path tested via direct module invocation with synthetic categorical param.

steps.action: Run search with --seed 42 twice to /tmp/search-test-1.json and /tmp/search-test-2.json
steps.observed: Candidates identical: all config values, scores, ranks, param_deltas match between runs
Different: e.g., iteration 1 config: seed=42 has swing_points.N.5m=3, seed=99 has swing_points.N.5m=4

evidence.terminalOutput: PASS: seed=42 produces identical candidates across two runs\nPASS: Different seeds produce different candidates\nExample: iter 1 config differs: seed=42 {swing_points.N.5m: 3}, seed=99 {swing_points.N.5m: 4}
evidence.diffOutput: Zero differences in candidates between seed=42 runs; non-zero differences between seed=42 and seed=99

frictions.description: All fitness scores are 0.0 because ground truth labels (3 labels) don't match any detections in the cascade output — the labels reference detection_ids not produced by the current dataset/config. This means score ranking is essentially arbitrary (all tied at 0). The search infrastructure works correctly but meaningful fitness differentiation requires labels that match actual detections.
frictions.resolution: Not a code bug — labels need to reference detections actually produced by the baseline config on this dataset. Testing was still conclusive for all assertions.

frictions.description: No categorical parameters exist in the production search_space.yaml. VAL-PERT-002 was tested by directly invoking the perturbation module with a synthetic categorical param.
frictions.resolution: Verified categorical code path via direct module test since production config only has numeric params.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All search CLI flags work correctly (--help, missing flag errors). Iterations run exact count (3/3). Output JSON has proper schema with ranked candidates (rank, score, config, iteration). Ctrl+C saves partial results as valid JSON with interrupted=true. Progress display shows [N/total] format with best score tracking and summary block. Numeric params stay within search-space bounds. Categorical perturbation confirmed via module test (no categorical params in prod config). Seed reproducibility verified: same seed = identical output, different seed = different output.
